definitions.text: A large, generally omnivorous mammal (a few species are purely carnivorous or herbivorous) of the family Ursidae; having shaggy fur, a very small tail, and flat feet. The meat of this animal.
examples: We had barbecued bear for dinner.
We had barbecued bear for dinner.

definitions.text: To endeavour to depress the price of, or prices in.
Characterized by declining prices in securities markets or by belief that the prices will fall.
examples: The great bear market starting in 1929 scared a whole generation of investors.

definitions.text: To carry or convey, literally or figuratively.
examples: Judging from the look on his face, he wasn't bearing good news.

definitions.text: To carry (weapons, flags or symbols of rank, office, etc.) upon one's person, especially visibly; to be equipped with (weapons, etc.).
